title: Has 30 Changed Your Views On the Previous Films
display_title: Has 30 Changed Your Views On the Previous Films
wikitext:
[]: [Spoilers] Has 3.0 Changed Your Views On the Previous Films? Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sat Mar 09, 2013 5:34 am I think by now we've all realized Shinji really is the central focus in Rebuild. While he was the main character in the original series, he was also pretty clearly a piece of a larger picture. With 3.0, certain decisions in the previous two films make a great deal of more sense to me, particularly the lesser focus on the smaller characters, such as Touji and Ritsuko. And some decisions, such as Asuka being the pilot of Eva-03 over Touji, make more sense to me now since Asuka has greater importance to Shinji than Touji in this narrative. While I'm still iffy on the execution (I need to give 1.11 and 2.22 a rewatch here in a few days), it's certainly more clear to me now what Anno was going for than before 3.0 was released. What about you, Eva Geeks Forum? Has 3.0 changed how you look at the previous two films? If so, how?

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Mar 09, 2013 6:13 am While the passage of time has increased my apathy towards 3.0, I feel that the sentiments I expressed in the immediate aftermath, (What was the point of Ha again?) vis-a-vis the light that Q casts on the earlier episodes, still hold.

[]: Post by pwhodges » Sat Mar 09, 2013 6:15 am Well, as you say, 3.0 has provided justification for a number of things in the earlier films that seemed like arbitrary changes when seen in isolation; however, I haven't felt the need to actually reinterpret anything in the them as a result of 3.0, apart from a better understanding of some of the nuances, for instance in the ending of 2.0. This process of looking back does reinforce the point, though, that there are likely to be matters in 3.0 which seem like puzzles now but will be explained more clearly when we see where they lead to in 4.0 (indeed, one could almost say that the whole of 3.0 is in that category!).

[]: Post by CJD » Sat Mar 09, 2013 6:22 am Yea, obviously with 3.0 we might have to look back and alter our interpretations of the previous two. 1.11 not so much I imagine (though I could be wrong, it's been a while since I watched it), but 2.22 definitely is a different film in retrospect. Assuming the tone shift between 2.22 and 3.0 was planned ahead of time, even if the plot wasn't (because we all know how Anno changes things up mid development), I think it's pretty obvious that the lighthearted tone of 2.22 was specifically designed to lure viewers into a false sense of security. If 2.22 had been just as dark and introspective as it's respective parts in NGE, I think going into 3.0 many viewers would have different expectations, darker expectations. Unfortunately for Anno, when you expect something it's much less shocking, and can be less emotional, than when you don't expect it. So how does Anno solve the problem of viewers expecting something dark, and thus not being shocked when it happens? Make them think this time will be different. Make 2.22 more lighthearted than it's counterparts in NGE. Then right when the viewer falls into your trap you pull the rug out from under them with 3.0. Bam! Well played Anno, well played. One could argue the downside is that he'll never get to pull this trick again, but fortunately for him he doesn't have to, not unless he Rebuilds Rebuild of Evangelion that is.

[]: Post by Azathoth » Sat Mar 09, 2013 2:14 pm Mr. Tines wrote: While the passage of time has increased my apathy towards 3.0, I feel that the sentiments I expressed in the immediate aftermath, (What was the point of Ha again?) vis-a-vis the light that Q casts on the earlier episodes, still hold.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Mar 09, 2013 2:27 pm Azathoth wrote: it's not because Anno had nothing further to say about Shinji and Rei's relationship

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 09, 2013 2:40 pm 3.0 definitely makes me look at the first two films in a slightly different light, but, since I was long on the "this won't end well" side where 2.0 is concerned, it hasn't brought about a paradigm shift or anything.

[]: Post by Atropos » Sat Mar 09, 2013 3:06 pm 3.0 definitely makes me look at the first two films in a slightly different light, but, since I was long on the "this won't end well" side where 2.0 is concerned, it hasn't brought about a paradigm shift or anything.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 09, 2013 3:08 pm I'm not sure what I think of people who'd change their views on the finale of 2.0 based on the fact that whoops, it did take. Because how far Kaworu's damage control works or didn't work (and judging by the trailer, the first plan was for it to work) is not related to what Shinji did, he was not involved in limiting or not limiting/ stopping or not stopping the impact. What he did inadvertedly caused boom, and wether someone intereferes in time, or just closes pandoras box in time to keep the last hope from dissipating is someone else's action, not Shinji's, what Shinji did or didn't do stays the same either way. Wether we get what the trailer seemed to suggest or what we actually saw depends on the mechanics of the spearing and those in charge of it, most likely SEELE, not on Shinji's actions themselves. If you're ready to say "He can't be blamed if he didn't know he could have destroyed the world", you should also be ready to say "He can't be blamed if he didn't know he actually destroyed the world", because even if he hadn't, he could have , and you either believe he neither wanted not knew about this (which is pretty much what Q shows us, if you ask me) or you don't. If you said he couldn't be blamed/took a step forward in a certain sense, you should be able to follow that to the final consequence. or you should have joined the anti camp in the first place, to begin with, that would be more honest. Or were you just in the pro-Shinji faction because the wholöe incidentit "looked badass"? I hope not. That's, frankly, not exactly the point of being a Shinji fan. What happened in 2.0 didn't change, just what we expected to happen afterwards.

[]: Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Mar 09, 2013 4:20 pm Not really. 3.0 felt so different from any version of Eva that I have ever encountered that it nearly felt like a different work altogether. I suppose it makes me appreciate the first two a little more, but that's about it.

[]: Post by Na7e » Sat Mar 09, 2013 5:52 pm Not really. I mean 3.0 got rid of my distaste for the rebuild in general. Since it reassured me that Anno wasn't just out to pander. I didn't actually think 3.0 was actually good it really was mediocre at best. The reaction to it was far more entertaining.

[]: Post by CJD » Sat Mar 09, 2013 6:57 pm Kendrix wrote: I'm not sure what I think of people who'd change their views on the finale of 2.0 based on the fact that whoops, it did take. .... If you said he couldn't be blamed/took a step forward in a certain sense, you should be able to follow that to the final consequence. or you should have joined the anti camp in the first place, to begin with, that would be more honest. Or were you just in the pro-Shinji faction because the wholöe incidentit "looked badass"? I hope not. That's, frankly, not exactly the point of being a Shinji fan. What happened in 2.0 didn't change, just what we expected to happen afterwards.

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 09, 2013 8:30 pm CJD wrote: Wait... are you talking to me?

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 09, 2013 8:44 pm Kendrix wrote: No, not you, I'm glad your existance as of the last supporters....

[]: Post by Rei IV » Sat Mar 09, 2013 9:43 pm Kendrix wrote: Uh... *suddenly weirdly self concious`* didn't mean you specifically, just in general... sort of. No, not you, I'm glad your existance as of the last supporters....

[]: Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 09, 2013 10:22 pm Reichu wrote: want the protagonist to come out of this mess a better and wiser person. .

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 09, 2013 10:30 pm Kendrix : If Shinji actually were a real guy, he probably wouldn't benefit much from the nonstop pity party you've been holding for him. You've been focusing too much on "woe is him" and "poor Shinji doesn't deserve this because X, Y, Z" and etc., and less on "so where does he go from here, and how does he get there?". Really... Thinking a bullet through his head seems like a viable option? I'd keep that kind of "support" far, far away from a guy like Shinji.

[]: Post by Rei IV » Sat Mar 09, 2013 10:42 pm Reichu wrote: Kendrix : If Shinji actually were a real guy, he probably wouldn't benefit much from the nonstop pity party you've been holding for him. You've been focusing too much on "woe is him" and "poor Shinji doesn't deserve this because X, Y, Z" and etc., and less on "so where does he go from here, and how does he get there?". Really... Thinking a bullet through his head seems like a viable option? I'd keep that kind of "support" far, far away from a guy like Shinji.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 09, 2013 10:46 pm Rei don't need no pity either. In fact, I bet she doesn't want it.

[]: Post by CJD » Sat Mar 09, 2013 10:52 pm Kendrix wrote: Uh... *suddenly weirdly self concious`* didn't mean you specifically, just in general... sort of.
